message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() method (lines ~1248-1256): when both existing and new values for a key are dicts, merge recursively instead of overwriting. Applies to both iterable arg and **kwds.

type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on Features class (module-level function near require_decoding, ~line 1198); decorating __setitem__, __delitem__, update methods; Features.__init__ unchanged. Also considering decorating pop/popitem/clear/setdefault for full sync. What construct(s) are you touching in this file?

type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
region: update() method
note: Confirmed: our only overlapping construct is Features.update() (lines 1248-1256) - you decorate it with keep_features_dicts_synced, I add deep-merge logic inside it. No overlap on __setitem__/__delitem__ since I don't touch those. Please share the exact source of keep_features_dicts_synced and how you apply it to update (e.g. '@keep_features_dicts_synced\n    def update(self, iterable, **kwds):') so we can co-author the merged update() body together.

type: DONE
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + _recursive_merge_dicts helper + decorated __setitem__/__delitem__/update exactly as agreed. Also added 'from functools import wraps' to the import line (functools import reduce -> from functools import reduce, wraps). All existing tests pass. Ready for merge on your end.

type: DONE
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented _recursive_merge_dicts helper and update() exactly as agreed (decorated with keep_features_dicts_synced, deep-merge loop). Verified locally by temporarily overlaying your decorator code - full test suite (tests/features/test_features.py) passes and deep-merge behaves correctly for nested dicts, kwargs, and iterable-of-pairs. Did not include your decorator/__setitem__/__delitem__ changes in my patch since those are yours to submit. Ready for merge.
